rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMOND L. MAR REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Antoinette Mallette And Veronica Ligne Mar For Summary Adjudication Of First Cause Of Action For Modification Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: The Court grants both of Respondents' Requests for Judicial Notice. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (21015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Respondents' evidentiary objections are overruled. Respondents' Motion for Summary Adjudication on the First Cause of Action to the Petition to Modify Trust and Compel Co-Trustees to Account, to Remove Co-Trustees, to Declare Deeds Void; for Redress from Breaches of Trust; to Enjoin Co-Trustees is denied. In support of the motion, Respondents rely primarily on Petitioner's discovery responses made prior to the October 5, 2020 court orders granting Respondents' motions to compel further responses. The Court granted these motions to compel further answers on a finding that the responses were insufficient. Petitioner's opposition to this motion relies primarily on his discovery responses made after the October 5, 2020 orders, as well as a declaration by the drafting attorney. Respondents urge the Court to disregard both, citing Whitmire v. Ingersoll-Rand Co. (2010) 184 Cal. App. 4th 1078 and lodging evidentiary objections. The circumstances here are not similar to Whitmire. In Whitmire, the relied-upon declaration contradicted responses produced in discovery from the same party. Here, the declarations submitted in support of the opposition to the motion do not contradict Petitioner's discovery responses produced after the October 5, 2020 orders compelling further responses to interrogatories and requests for admission. Further, although a moving party may establish its prima facie entitlement to summary adjudication by demonstrating that an opponent's discovery responses are devoid of supportive evidence, merely evasive or incomplete answers to written discovery are not legally sufficient grounds for granting a motion for summary judgment or adjudication when other evidence presented on the motion shows there are triable issues of material fact. (See Ahn v. Kumho Tire U.S.A., Inc. (2014) 166 Cal.Rptr.3d 852.) Here, the evidence presented in support of and in opposition to the motion for summary adjudication of the Petition's First Cause of Action shows there are triable issues of material fact regarding the settlor's knowledge, awareness, and intentions in establishing the trust at the time the instrument was drafted and executed. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on November 18, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.
